title: Citrusy Crispy Egyptian Okra Fritters
description: Light, zesty okra fritters that combine the crunch of a fried snack with bright citrus flavors, peppercorn warmth, and a hint of cumin.
instructions: Wash and trim the okra, then cut into 1/2-inch pieces.
In a medium bowl whisk together chickpea flour, all‑purpose flour, baking powder, ground black pepper, cumin, paprika, and salt until well combined.
Add lemon zest, orange zest, crushed peppercorns, and the juice of the lemon; stir to form a thick batter.
Toss the sliced okra in the batter, ensuring each piece is coated. Let it rest for 10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.

Heat about 2 inches of oil in a deep‑sided skillet or pot to 180 °C (350 °F). Fry the coated okra pieces in batches, turning once, until each side is golden brown and crispy, about 2–3 minutes per side.
Remove the fritters with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. Serve immediately with an extra squeeze of fresh lemon juice.
Optionally, sprinkle a few extra crushed peppercorns on top for a peppery finish.
